rulings.Case Title: JOHN WILLIAM THOMPSON et al VS. 3M COMPANY et al
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, August 28, 2014 in Department 503, Line 2. Defendant The Sherwin-Williams Company's motion for summary adjudication of plaintiffs' claim for punitive damages will be continued pursuant to plaintiffs' C.C.P. 437c(h) request for a continuance, which is granted. A hearing is required to discuss supplemental briefing and a new hearing date. The hearing will be at 9:45a.m. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. = (503/TLJ)
